prompt:
hey! for the museum's escape-room kiosk we're doing a tiny text adventure, and I got voluntold to ship the engine. I sketched the content format and the exact player-facing strings with the writers (they are VERY picky about wording, please match them byte for byte) — can you build the engine + parser? plain TypeScript, node only, no packages. two modules the tests import:

parser.ts — export `parse(input)` returning a command object. normalize first: trim, collapse runs of whitespace to one space, lowercase everything. then:
- '' -> { kind: 'empty' }
- bare n/s/e/w or north/south/east/west -> { kind: 'go', direction: '<full name>' } (only those eight get the bare treatment — bare 'down' is NOT a move, it's unknown)
- 'go <anything>' -> { kind: 'go', direction: '<rest>' } — 'go' alone is unknown
- 'look' or 'l' -> { kind: 'look' }
- 'inventory', 'inv' or 'i' -> { kind: 'inventory' }
- 'take <item>', 'get <item>', 'pick up <item>' -> { kind: 'take', item: '<item>' } (items can be multi-word, e.g. 'brass key'); bare 'take'/'get' with no item is unknown
- 'drop <item>' -> { kind: 'drop', item }
- anything else -> { kind: 'unknown', input: '<normalized input>' }

engine.ts — export `class Game`. `new Game(world)` where world = { start, win: { room, item }, rooms }. each room: { name, description, exits, items? }. an exit value is either a room id string or { to, requires, lockedMessage? } — requires names an item the player must be CARRYING to pass (it is not consumed). construction must validate start, win.room and every exit target against the room table and throw Error 'unknown room "<id>"' on a miss. API: `execute(input) -> string`, `location() -> room id`, `inventory() -> sorted item names (a copy)`, `isWon() -> boolean`.

the strings (writer-approved, exact):
- entering a room (successful go) and 'look' both return the room description block:
  line 1 room name, line 2 description, then 'You see: <items sorted, comma+space>.' only when the room has items, then 'Exits: <directions sorted>.' or 'Exits: none.'
- no exit that way -> 'You can't go <direction>.'
- exit locked (requires not carried) -> the exit's lockedMessage, or 'The way <direction> is locked.' when it has none
- take ok -> 'You take the <item>.' / item not here -> 'There is no <item> here.'
- drop ok -> 'You drop the <item>.' / not carried -> 'You aren't carrying a <item>.'
- inventory -> 'You are carrying: <sorted, comma+space>.' or 'You are carrying nothing.'
- empty input -> 'Say something.' / unknown -> 'I don't understand "<normalized input>".'
- win: the moment the player is in win.room with win.item in inventory (check after every successful go/take/drop), append '\n\nYou have won!' to that command's response. from then on every execute returns 'The adventure is over.'

sample of the vibe (our pitch world — bring the idol back to the entrance):

> go down
An iron gate blocks the stairs.
> n
The Library
Shelves sag under mouldy books.
You see: brass key, dusty tome.
Exits: south.
> take brass key
You take the brass key.

take/drop move items between the room and your hands, so 'look' reflects what's been picked up. acceptance is `node --test test_parser.ts test_engine.ts` — those two files are the writers' contract, do not edit them. how you factor the internals (world validation, room rendering, etc.) is up to you.
